expectations.text: Review includes a complexity/split advisory (3 layers: Core, API, Frontend across 27+ files)
expectations.evidence: The review includes: '> [!NOTE] > **Complexity advisory** -- This PR may benefit from splitting. > - **Layer spread:** 3 layers touched (Core, API, Frontend) across 27 reviewable files. Consider splitting by layer.'

expectations.text: Flags new interface methods without default implementations as breaking changes (Pattern 3)
expectations.evidence: Two Critical findings: 'IUserService.UpdateCurrentUserAsync added to public interface without a default implementation... Pattern 3 violation' and 'IUserPresentationFactory.CreateUpdateCurrentUserModelAsync added to public interface without a default implementation. Same Pattern 3 violation.'

expectations.text: Review does not flag purely cosmetic/stylistic issues unless they affect performance or rendering. Missing JSDoc on new public APIs is NOT a stylistic issue -- it is a legitimate finding.
expectations.evidence: No cosmetic/stylistic findings. The JSDoc suggestion for the public save() method is explicitly allowed by the assertion. Other findings are about missing validation, AbortController bugs, memory leaks, and missing tests.

expectations.text: All findings reference code that was changed in the diff, not pre-existing issues
expectations.evidence: All findings reference new code introduced in the PR: new UpdateCurrentUserAsync method, new repository methods, new modal element, new SectionUserNoPermission condition. No pre-existing issues are flagged.

expectations.text: Review assesses the new feature's architecture, patterns, or integration approach -- not just absence of bugs
expectations.evidence: The review intro describes the feature's architecture (new condition, workspace modal, API endpoints). The suggestions assess integration patterns (AbortController sharing, missing test for condition, JSDoc on public API). The breaking change findings assess how the feature integrates with existing interfaces.

expectations.text: Review does NOT flag UpdateCurrentUserAsync as missing UserSavingNotification/UserSavedNotification -- the sibling UpdateAsync also does not publish these notifications, so flagging their absence would be a false positive
expectations.evidence: No mention of 'notification', 'Notification', 'SavedNotification', or 'SavingNotification' anywhere in the review. The review does not discuss notification publishing at all.

eval_feedback.overall: Perfect pass. The with_skill review correctly identifies all key issues including Pattern 3 violations on both interfaces, includes complexity advisory, and avoids false positives.
